scenario_results.evidence: Manifest scope and Microsoft, Google, Atlassian, and Slack role sources support bounded SaaS admin-role delegation guidance.
Supported tasks and authority sources cover tenant-side entitlement cleanup while explicitly rejecting procurement and contract ownership.
App approval, marketplace governance, and third-party access controls are supported with clear refusal of custom integration implementation.
Manifest and source baseline support SaaS-side sharing and app-access policy alignment while routing identity and compliance depth elsewhere.
Audit-log and admin-action review is directly supported by official Atlassian, Google, and Slack operational sources.
Vendor release and message-center sources support staged rollout, communications, and rollback guidance for SaaS changes.
Native admin automation is bounded to access and guardrail decisions and does not widen into process redesign or engineering.
Seat reclamation and SaaS-side offboarding actions remain inside tenant administration while routing identity and HR policy elsewhere.
Plan or SKU limitation handling is explicit, source-backed, and conservative about unsupported workarounds.
Out-of-scope rules clearly reject scripts, middleware, and product code for lifecycle or policy automation.
Adjacent specialties are named and the manifest requires partial handling plus explicit handoff to CRM, document-management, and identity specialists.
The package requests missing tenant context, preserves the local reviewed baseline, and defines safe fallback for stale delegated outputs.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against issue #145 requirements, including SaaS administration, governance, audit handling, workflow guardrails, edge-case constraints, out-of-scope rejection, low-confidence escalation, and shared-meta-agent fallback behavior.
No unreviewed regressions remain and human verification is recorded in readiness evidence.
